content:
Hi :wave: I have rebased the tyr-for-upstream branch onto drm-rust-next which now includes v7.0-rc1

This is a forced update to tyr-for-upstream so it won't pull cleanly.

If you have any build or rustfmt issues please let me know.

Hi Deborah, the formatting part is fixed but it's still failing from the new fw module:

error[E0433]: failed to resolve: could not find `firmware` in `kernel`

Here's my config:

$ make LLVM=1 defconfig
./scripts/config \
  --set-str LOCALVERSION "-lkdev" \
  --enable RUST \
  --enable DRM \
  --enable DRM_TYR \
  --enable NFS_FS \
  --enable NFS_V4 \
  --enable ROOT_NFS \
  --enable IP_PNP \
  --enable IP_PNP_DHCP \
  --enable STMMAC_ETH \
  --enable STMMAC_PLATFORM \
  --enable DWMAC_ROCKCHIP \
  --enable PHYLIB \
  --enable MDIO_BUS \
  --enable REALTEK_PHY

I am compiling for aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu- btw (export ARCH="arm64" && export CROSS_COMPILE="aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-").
@Onur Özkan thank you for reporting this. It looks like i dropped "depends on RUST_FW_LOADER_ABSTRACTIONS" from "drivers/gpu/drm/tyr/Kconfig". I will add it back into the fw-boot series.

Hi I've forced an update to tyr-for-upstream onto drm-rust-next. There is a change to watch out for here:
In previous drafts we had a struct in tyr/driver.rs named TyrPlatformDeviceData but it's now renamed TyrPlatformDriverData
I've force an update to tyr-for-upstream to rebase on drm-rust-next and to use the latest v8 of [PATCH v8 0/7] Rust bindings for gem shmem + iosys_map

I've forced a pretty large update to tyr-for-upstream which now includes:

  • drm-rust-next at Linux 7.0-rc4 and the register! macro
  • [PATCH v9 0/7] Rust bindings for gem shmem
  • [PATCH v[6] 0/4] Introduce DeviceContext
  • [PATCH v2 0/12] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support

Another update for tyr-for-upstream which now includes:

  • drm-rust-next with the DRM shmem helper abstraction
  • [PATCH v5 0/6] Rust GPUVM immediate mode

  • [PATCH v6 0/5] Introduce DeviceContext

  • [PATCH v2 0/12] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support with just some minimal changes to adapt to updated abstractions
I've forced an update to tyr-for-upstream to make use of the register! macro for our register definitions.

I've updated tyr-for-upstream again since I've sent a better version of the register macro to the mailing list:

  • [PATCH v4 0/6] drm/tyr: Use register! macro

@Daniel Almeida Hi Daniel, I submitted a new MR to add VM- and BO-related ioctl support for Tyr. I’ve already run the relevant Panthor GEM and VM test cases with igt-gpu-tools. I'd appreciate it if you could review it when you have time.

https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/64

I've force pushed an update to tyr-for-upstream which includes:

  • drm-rust-next @ drm-rust-next-2026-04-06
  • [PATCH v5 0/6] drm/tyr: Use register! macro
  • [PATCH v6 0/5] Rust GPUVM immediate mode
  • [PATCH v10 0/5] Rust bindings for gem shmem

Forced update to tyr-for-upstream which includes:

  • [PATCH v3 00/13] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support

@Deborah Brouwer The code has been modified according to your review suggestions and rebased onto the latest tyr-for-upstream branch.

https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/64
A small update can be fetched for tyr-for-upstream. I added a temporary patch to hard-code FEAT_RENDER so we can run the igt-gpu-tools tests on vm/bo ioctl support.

New forced update to tyr-for-upstream includes:

  • patch to remove DRIVER_GEM_GPUVA flag
  • patch to add all global CSF interfaces using the register! macro

Deborah Brouwer said:

New forced update to tyr-for-upstream includes:

  • patch to remove DRIVER_GEM_GPUVA flag
  • patch to add all global CSF interfaces using the register! macro

On my NanoPi R6C development board, Tyr reports an error:

$ sudo dmesg | grep tyr
[ 1.745058] tyr fb000000.gpu: supply sram not found, using dummy regulator
[ 1.745357] tyr fb000000.gpu: mali-g610 GPU_ID 0xa8670005 major 0x0 minor 0x0 status 0x5
[ 1.745364] tyr fb000000.gpu: Features: L2:0x7120306 Tiler:0x809 Mem:0x301 MMU:0x2830 AS:0xff
[ 1.745368] tyr fb000000.gpu: shader_present=0x0000000000050005 l2_present=0x0000000000000001 tiler_present=0x0000000000000001
[ 1.745834] tyr: Firmware protected mode entry not supported, ignoring
[ 1.748029] tyr: CSF interface version is 0. Firmware may have failed to boot.
[ 1.749910] tyr fb000000.gpu: probe with driver tyr failed with error -22

@Alvin Sun thanks for this report, is the problem that the address is wrong? or that we are trying to read it too quickly? could you poll for glb_control.read(GLB_VERSION) and see what happens? did you try any other debugging?

Deborah Brouwer said:

Alvin Sun thanks for this report, is the problem that the address is wrong? or that we are trying to read it too quickly? could you poll for glb_control.read(GLB_VERSION) and see what happens? did you try any other debugging?

Yes, reading GLB_VERSION right after firmware::boot() was too early; the firmware hadn't prepared the global interface yet. I tried adding a JOB_IRQ_RAWSTAT GLB status check inside boot(), and then the test worked normally.

@@ -288,8 +280,8 @@ pub(crate) fn boot(&self) -> Result {
         io.write_reg(MCU_CONTROL::zeroed().with_req(McuControlMode::Auto));

         if let Err(e) = poll::read_poll_timeout(
-            || Ok(io.read(MCU_STATUS)),
-            |status| status.value() == McuStatus::Enabled,
+            || Ok((io.read(MCU_STATUS), io.read(JOB_IRQ_RAWSTAT))),
+            |(mcu_status, irq_rawstat)| mcu_status.value() == McuStatus::Enabled && irq_rawstat.glb(),
             time::Delta::from_millis(1),
             time::Delta::from_millis(100),
         ) {

Alvin Sun said:

Deborah Brouwer said:

Alvin Sun thanks for this report, is the problem that the address is wrong? or that we are trying to read it too quickly? could you poll for glb_control.read(GLB_VERSION) and see what happens? did you try any other debugging?

Yes, reading GLB_VERSION right after firmware::boot() was too early; the firmware hadn't prepared the global interface yet. I tried adding a JOB_IRQ_RAWSTAT GLB status check inside boot(), and then the test worked normally.

@@ -288,8 +280,8 @@ pub(crate) fn boot(&self) -> Result {
         io.write_reg(MCU_CONTROL::zeroed().with_req(McuControlMode::Auto));

         if let Err(e) = poll::read_poll_timeout(
-            || Ok(io.read(MCU_STATUS)),
-            |status| status.value() == McuStatus::Enabled,
+            || Ok((io.read(MCU_STATUS), io.read(JOB_IRQ_RAWSTAT))),
+            |(mcu_status, irq_rawstat)| mcu_status.value() == McuStatus::Enabled && irq_rawstat.glb(),
             time::Delta::from_millis(1),
             time::Delta::from_millis(100),
         ) {

phew ok i'm working on adding wait and job irq right now so we can wait on these register reads properly soon.

Forced update for tyr-for-upstream includes:

  • [PATCH v12 0/5] Rust bindings for gem shmem
  • [PATCH v4 00/20] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support

Deborah Brouwer said:

Forced update for tyr-for-upstream includes:

  • [PATCH v12 0/5] Rust bindings for gem shmem
  • [PATCH v4 00/20] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support

@Alvin Sun please test if you can
@Deborah Brouwer I have tested this branch on my R6C SBC, and it works.
Update to tyr-for-upstream to rebase on drm-rust-next which now includes v7.1-rc1

Update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • rebase on drm-rust-next and
  • add [PATCH v13 0/5] Rust bindings for gem shmem

Update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • rebase on drm-rust-next
  • add [PATCH v2 0/7] rust: drm: Higher-Ranked Lifetime private data
  • add [PATCH v19 0/4] Rust bindings for gem shmem
  • Some fixes to make the shmem tests work with new drm::Driver RegistrationData and ParentDevice types
  • Some temporary fixes to gpuvm and pagetables that still need some work https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/work_items/89
  • Removed Tyr irqs until they can be adapted to lifetimes

@Laura Nao and I have updated  tyr-for-upstream to:

  • rebase on drm-rust-next
  • update [PATCH v4 00/16] rust: drm: Higher-Ranked Lifetime private data
  • update [PATCH v3 1/7] rust: types: rename ForLt to CovariantForLt
  • some temporary fixes to make the above two series work with drm-rust-next
  • use [PATCH v21 4/4] rust: drm: gem: Introduce shmem::Object::sg_table() even though this sg_table() function will likely change

Forced update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • rebase on drm-rust-next which now includes [PATCH v21 4/4] rust: drm: gem: Introduce shmem::Object::sg_table() thanks to @Lyude

Forced update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • rebase on drm-rust-next which now includes v7.2-rc1
  • add the lifetime patch for page tables [PATCH] rust: iommu: add device lifetime to IoPageTable
  • add the latest private data series from @Danilo Krummrich [PATCH v5 00/19] rust: drm: Higher-Ranked Lifetime private data

Forced update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • add the latest lifetime patch for page tables [PATCH v3] rust: iommu: add device lifetime to IoPageTable
  • add the newest firmware booting series [PATCH v5 0/7] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support

(cc @Deborah Brouwer ) Forced update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • Add the newest firmware booting series [PATCH v6 0/7] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support
  • Add patch that adapts IRQ registration to work with the HRT driver architecture rust: irq: make Registration compatible with lifetime-bound drivers
  • Add support for IRQs on Tyr, adapted to work with the new HRT architecture
    - drm/tyr: add Wait type for GPU events
    - drm/tyr: add Job IRQ handling
    - drm/tyr: wait for global interface readiness

Forced update to tyr-for-upstream to:

  • Add the newest firmware booting series [PATCH v7 0/7] drm/tyr: firmware loading and MCU boot support
